Ensure the directory /etc/audit/rules.d exists.\n2. Ensure the rule file exists (touch if missing).\n3. If the rule line is not already present in the file, append it.\n4. Set permissions to 600 and ownership to root:root.\n\nThis approach avoids duplicating the rule in /etc/audit/audit.rules, keeps the audit configuration minimal, and preserves the default augenrules loading mechanism. No restart of auditd is performed; the rule will be applied automatically on the next auditd restart or when augenrules loads new rules.\n"}
